task_completion.description: Agent writes code to generate Master Dark and Master Flat frames using median-combination.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script open in an editor showing logic to read multiple FITS files from the 'darks/' and 'flats/' directories and combine them using a median function (e.g., numpy.median).

task_completion.description: Agent writes code to identify hot and dead pixels using the specified mathematical thresholds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing the exact formulas for defect identification: hot pixels as > (median + 5 * standard deviation) of the Master Dark, and dead pixels as < (0.5 * median) of the Master Flat.

task_completion.description: Agent executes the script to process the CCD frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing the execution of the Python (or other language) script, with no unhandled errors.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the defect report to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or file explorer showing the creation of 'defect_report.txt' in the '~/AstroImages/defect_mapping/' directory.

task_completion.description: The defect report contains all required labeled values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'defect_report.txt' displayed on screen (e.g., via 'cat' or a text editor) containing the exact labels: HOT_PIXEL_THRESHOLD, HOT_PIXEL_COUNT, DEAD_PIXEL_THRESHOLD, DEAD_PIXEL_COUNT, MASTER_DARK_MEDIAN, MASTER_DARK_STDDEV, MASTER_FLAT_MEDIAN, TOTAL_PIXELS, and DEFECT_FRACTION.

integrity.description: Agent computed the statistics programmatically rather than hardcoding fake values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script contains actual calculation logic using libraries like numpy and astropy.io.fits, rather than just printing pre-written numbers to a text file.

integrity.description: Agent used the provided FITS files for calculations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code explicitly loads files from the '~/AstroImages/defect_mapping/darks/' and '~/AstroImages/defect_mapping/flats/' directories.

integrity.description: The report file was genuinely saved to the filesystem.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the file existing on disk (e.g., via 'ls' or a file explorer) at '/home/ga/AstroImages/defect_mapping/defect_report.txt', rather than just being printed to the console.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses real astronomical CCD data from the Large Format Camera (LFC) on the Palomar 200-inch Hale Telescope (Zenodo record 3254683). The final report must be saved exactly to /home/ga/AstroImages/defect_mapping/defect_report.txt.
